# Glide

> Glide turns a phone into a wireless trackpad, keyboard and volume remote for a Windows or macOS PC. It runs over WebRTC on the local WiFi network: peer-to-peer, PIN-protected and DTLS-encrypted. No cables, no port forwarding, no certificates to accept.

## Facts

- Platforms: Windows and macOS (PC server), any modern mobile browser (Safari 16.4+, Chrome/Edge 111+, Firefox 128+) for the phone client.
- Connection: WebRTC data channel negotiated through a signaling server, which only relays a few KB of SDP/ICE metadata and never sees trackpad input or the PIN.
- Requirement: both devices must be on the same WiFi network (no out-of-LAN connectivity yet).
- License: GPL-3.0-or-later.
- Price: free.

## Docs

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/iMxSquash/glide)
- [Latest release](https://github.com/iMxSquash/glide/releases/latest)
- [License](https://github.com/iMxSquash/glide/blob/main/LICENSE)
